response.justification: Bogdan has genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map, which provides some domain adjacency, but the role fundamentally requires modern C++ or Rust proficiency and the candidate's primary stack is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month role over a decade ago and a bachelor's thesis project; there is no Rust experience and no routing or navigation pipeline work. The distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby is relevant in spirit but built on DLang, not the required systems languages, and does not involve geospatial routing concerns.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through GISCollective platform and Green Map GIS application development
Demonstrated experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed micro-services (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Strong quality and TDD culture — consistent application of software craftsmanship practices across roles
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over 10 years old; no Rust experience whatsoever — disqualifying given the primary tech requirement
No routing, navigation guidance, or map tile pipeline experience; geospatial work is data collection/display, not routing tiling
Primary expertise is web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React), which is misaligned with Linux systems-level C++ pipeline work
